extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won the sprint despite launching late from behind, beating VIVIANI Elia who led out too early and faded in final meters.
tactical_patterns: ACKERMANN launched sprint after others committed, showing power sprinters can win from behind when leadouts start at 700m not 300m.
CICCONE Giulio attacked on early uncategorized climb to extend KOM jersey gap, using non-points climbs tactically for jersey defense.
Lotto-Soudal's leadout collapsed after crash inside 1km, yet DÉMARE still contested sprint, revealing individual capacity overcomes lost positioning.
MCCARTHY Jay led positioning for MAJKA Rafał at 3.5km despite flat finish, suggesting GC teams feared late technical crashes more than energy cost.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: first Grand Tour, won despite late launch position, validates selection over Bennett as primary sprinter
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: led sprint but faded late, suggesting either too early launch or insufficient top-end power against pure sprinters
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: used uncategorized climb to pad KOM lead, showing tactical maturity to attack where points aren't available
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: contested sprint despite teammate crash at 1km, demonstrating exceptional bike handling and sprint resilience
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: neutral: positioned at 3.5km by McCarthy on flat stage, confirms team protecting GC placement not just mountains
